patterns_recognized: HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD-or-higher) is the archive's single most validated, non-negotiable cap -- twice confirmed correctly predicting Sale/ATL losses (6/4, 7/10) even against elite pre-cap signal stacks. Five picks tonight (MIL@PIT G1+G2, NYY@WSH, KC@BAL, ARI@LAD) carry agreeing SP-xERA/PQI signals but a negative real edge; none are rescued upward despite strong secondary confirmation.

The HARD CAP 10 four-dimension-affirm rescue (F-signal + SP-xERA agree + PQI confirm + Stage 1/2 agree, all simultaneously at pre-cap score>=5) validated twice (6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 CLE@MIA) applies ONLY when CAP 10 fires alone. MIL@PIT G2 hits that exact profile (pre-cap score=5) but HARD CAP 1 fires first on the same -0.63pp market edge as G1, so the rescue does not apply -- CAP 1 outranks it.

HARD CAP 7 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage) broke its 0-for-7 backtest on 7/10 KC@BAL at a marginal +0.018 gap, prompting a proposed 0.04 'meaningful' retuning threshold. COL@SF fires CAP 7 tonight at +0.0371 -- closer to that line than the break was, and n=1 is too thin to relax a 7-hit cap, but this pairing is a case to keep on the 30-pick tracking log.

CLE@MIA and ATL@STL are each rematches of games in yesterday's (7/10) slate where the OPPOSITE side was the graded pick (CLE was a CAP-10-rescued PLATINUM that won 3-2; ATL was a DIAMOND that CAP 1 correctly killed before a 1-2 loss). Tonight both matchups show fresh, reversed signal profiles -- do not carry yesterday's rescue or fade forward without re-deriving from tonight's own SP-xERA/PQI reads.

Six SKIP-tier picks (SEA@TB, CLE@MIA, HOU@TEX, CHC@CIN, ATL@STL, TOR@SD) carry the dual-opposition SP-xERA-against + PQI-against signature the archive has repeatedly flagged as a reliable opposing-team predictor (7/9 ATL@PIT, KC@NYM, CHC@BAL all lost directionally under this exact stack). OAK@CHW carries the same opposition despite a stale CSV tier of GOLD, matching the 7/9 OAK@DET stale-tier pattern -- DOWNGRADE applied there.

MIL @ PIT (G1).reasoning: Pre-cap A- (score=4): SP edge 0.79 xERA (Sproat over Ashcraft) agrees, PQI confirms (Delta=-15.4), Stage 1/2 agree. Real edge is only -0.63pp, tripping HARD CAP 1, then HARD CAP 10 stacks (runtime pick_prob=0.538, no CONFIRM anchor) to zero. Sale/ATL precedent shows CAP 1 is non-negotiable even against agreeing SP/PQI signals. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 non-negotiable on agreeing SP/PQI stacks

MIL @ PIT (G2).reasoning: Pre-cap A (score=5): F-signal fires, SP edge agrees, PQI confirms, Stage agrees -- the exact 4-dimension profile that rescued 6/12 ARI@CIN and 7/10 CLE@MIA from CAP 10 alone. But HARD CAP 1 fires first on the same -0.63pp market edge shared with G1, and CAP 1 outranks the rescue pattern (which only applies when CAP 10 fires without CAP 1). CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 10 four-dimension rescue does not override HARD CAP 1
6/12 ARI@CIN, 7/10 CLE@MIA rescue precedent

LAA @ MIN.reasoning: Large negative edge (-10.44pp) caps grade at C, Stage 1/2 disagree sharply (f5_full_delta=0.239, a genuine negative signal here, not a confirming one), and HARD CAP 10 stacks on top. This is a double-suppression with a disagreeing Stage signal -- not the 4-dimension affirm pattern, so no rescue case applies. acute_roster and bullpen_fatigue flags add caution. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: Stage 1/2 disagreement blocks CAP-10 rescue eligibility

OAK @ CHW.reasoning: CSV still tags this GOLD, but the underlying grade is D (score=-1): SP edge 0.66 xERA runs AGAINST CHW (Jump/OAK favored instead) and PQI runs -10.3 against as well; only F-signal and Stage agreement favor CHW. This mirrors the 7/9 OAK@DET stale-tier pattern (CSV label hadn't caught up to the opposing-signal read). Lineups are locked at first pitch, so this read is live-accurate. DOWNGRADE GOLD to SKIP.
lessons_applied: Stale-tier + opposing SP/PQI signature (7/9 OAK@DET precedent)

COL @ SF.reasoning: Pre-cap score=8 (clears every gate, F-signal, SP edge 1.65 xERA agrees, PQI confirms Delta=+24.0, Stage agree) is the strongest raw stack on the slate. HARD CAP 7 demotes it anyway: hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0371, a pick-side pen disadvantage. Yesterday's postgame showed this exact cap breaking its 0-for-7 streak at a smaller +0.018 gap, but n=1 is too thin to relax a 7-hit cap, and 0.0371 is closer to the proposed 0.04 retuning line. CONFIRM the demotion; keep on the tracking log.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 7 pick-side bullpen disadvantage (0-for-7 through 7/10, one recent break under review)

NYY @ WSH.reasoning: CSV pick column reads NYY -- reading directly, not inferring from full_prob=0.4137 (a home-team/WSH number that would mislead toward WSH). SP edge 2.02 xERA (Schlittler over Poulin) agrees, PQI confirms (Delta=-34.6). Real edge is -9.0pp, tripping HARD CAP 1, then HARD CAP 10 (runtime pick_prob=0.547) zeroes further. Same matchup on 7/10 (different WSH starter) was CONFIRMed at PLATINUM and NYY won 5-3 -- supportive but not enough to override CAP 1's non-negotiable status. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: Read the pick column literally, never derive from f5_prob/full_prob (7/8 misread lesson)
HARD CAP 1 non-negotiable

BOS @ NYM.reasoning: Genuine data gap per the CSV: Eduardo Rivera has only 17 Statcast pitches season-to-date, well below the 100-pitch xERA-scoring minimum, so no pick can be graded. No live-lineup verification was attempted this run. CONFIRM the pending state; nothing to override.
lessons_applied: PENDING_SP_DATA hard stop correctly withholds ungraded games

SEA @ TB.reasoning: D-grade: SP edge 1.48 xERA (Gilbert over Jax) runs against the TB pick, PQI also against -- the dual-opposition signature the archive has repeatedly validated as a loss predictor (7/9 ATL@PIT, KC@NYM, CHC@BAL). Note: this same matchup on 7/10 (different game in the series) was a similar C-grade SKIP where TB won 7-2, an unresolved Stage 1/2 ambiguity the archive explicitly flags as still open. That doesn't change tonight's signal opposition. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: SP-xERA-against + PQI-against dual-opposition reliable loss predictor
Stage 1/2 disagreement zone remains genuinely ambiguous, don't over-read either direction

CLE @ MIA.reasoning: D-grade: SP edge 0.61 xERA (Perez over Bibee) runs against the CLE pick, PQI +18.0 against, and fair_prob (0.397) sits below the 0.42 floor. This is a materially different signal profile from yesterday's 7/10 CLE@MIA, where all four dimensions affirmed CLE and the CAP-10 rescue paid off (3-2 win) -- tonight the signals have flipped against CLE. Do not carry yesterday's rescue forward. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: SP-xERA-against + PQI-against dual-opposition reliable loss predictor
Don't carry a prior day's rescue into a fresh signal profile

PHI @ DET.reasoning: Pre-cap B- (score=1): F3 signal fires but PQI runs -4.6 against DET, and only Stage agreement adds support -- short of the 4-dimension affirm bar needed for a CAP-10 rescue (unlike MIL@PIT G2 tonight). Edge (+15.48pp) also sits just outside the [4,15]pp band. Same matchup won 10-2 for DET on 7/10 as an unstaked SKIP, but that's not enough to rescue this thinner stack. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: CAP-10 rescue requires all four dimensions, not just F-signal + Stage agreement

KC @ BAL.reasoning: Pre-cap B+ (score=3): F-signal fires, SP edge 1.02 xERA (Bradish over Cameron) agrees, PQI confirms (Delta=+19.7). Real edge is -9.65pp, tripping HARD CAP 1, then HARD CAP 10 (runtime pick_prob=0.533) zeroes it. Note hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0181 for BAL is nearly identical to the +0.018 gap that broke HARD CAP 7's streak on this exact matchup yesterday (BAL won 5-3) -- but CAP 1 is the operative block here, not CAP 7, and CAP 1's non-negotiable status takes priority. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 non-negotiable on agreeing SP/PQI stacks

HOU @ TEX.reasoning: D-grade: SP edge 1.04 xERA (Lambert over Rocker) runs against the TEX pick, PQI -18.1 against, and the real edge is essentially flat (-0.23pp). Textbook dual-opposition SKIP. CONFIRM.
lessons_applied: SP-xERA-against + PQI-against dual-opposition reliable loss predictor

CHC @ CIN.reasoning: D-grade: SP edge 1.47 xERA (Assad over Lodolo) and PQI (Delta=-18.5) both run against the CIN pick; the team-quality modifier also points away (disabled). Real edge is flat (-0.44pp). Clean triple-opposition SKIP. CONFIRM.
lessons_applied: SP-xERA-against + PQI-against dual-opposition reliable loss predictor

ATL @ STL.reasoning: D-grade: SP edge 1.00 xERA (Lopez over Liberatore) and PQI (Delta=-21.5) both run against the STL pick; edge (+15.30pp) sits just outside the [4,15]pp band. Yesterday's ATL@STL had ATL as a DIAMOND that HARD CAP 1 correctly killed before a 1-2 loss -- tonight the pick flips to STL with its own opposing-signal profile. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: SP-xERA-against + PQI-against dual-opposition reliable loss predictor
Don't carry a prior day's read into a rematch without re-deriving

TOR @ SD.reasoning: D-grade: SP edge 1.36 xERA (Yesavage over Buehler) and PQI (Delta=-21.2) both run against the SD pick; team-quality modifier also opposes (disabled). acute_roster stress flag adds further caution. Clean triple-opposition SKIP. CONFIRM.
lessons_applied: SP-xERA-against + PQI-against dual-opposition reliable loss predictor

ARI @ LAD.reasoning: Pre-cap B+ (score=3): F1_xera_gap=1.67* is thin-sample-starred but corroborated by F2 (0.040) and F3 (13767.8, the largest on the slate), plus PQI confirms (Delta=+19.6) and SP edge agrees (1.12, Yamamoto over Pfaadt) -- satisfying the 'F1* needs secondary confirmation' rule from the 7/9 NYY@TB lesson. Still, real edge is -19.44pp, tripping HARD CAP 1 then HARD CAP 10 to zero. CAP 1 remains non-negotiable regardless of corroborated signals. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: F1* requires F2/F3/PQI corroboration to carry directional weight (7/9 NYY@TB lesson)
HARD CAP 1 non-negotiable
